seasonal_wine.name: Chianti Classico Collection, mid February
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, second or third week of February
seasonal_wine.description: The Chianti Classico Consortium gathers more than 200 producers at Stazione Leopolda in Florence to pour the latest vintage of annata, Riserva and Gran Selezione across two days. The opening day is trade-only, the second opens to the public.
seasonal_wine.tip: Public day sells out in advance once tickets release in January; consortium members and press get priority access on day one.

seasonal_wine.name: Anteprima della Vernaccia di San Gimignano, mid February
seasonal_wine.description: Inside the Museo Civico in the heart of San Gimignano, the Consorzio della Vernaccia presents the newest DOCG vintage to press, trade and consumers. Tuscany's only DOCG white opens the Anteprime week.
seasonal_wine.tip: Pair the tasting with a climb up the Torre Grossa for a panoramic view of the vine-laced hills that produced the wines in the glass.

seasonal_wine.name: Anteprima del Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, mid February
seasonal_wine.description: Inside the Fortezza di Montepulciano the Consorzio del Vino Nobile presents the new annata, the Riserva and the recently introduced Pieve tier. The 2026 edition brought a record 57 producers; the wine show is preceded by a press-only stars-of-the-vintage ceremony.
seasonal_wine.tip: The new Pieve sub-zone tier debuted in 2024 and ages 36 months minimum from estate fruit; ask producers to pour Pieve alongside annata to taste the village contrast.

seasonal_wine.name: Benvenuto Brunello, late February
seasonal_wine.description: The Consorzio del Vino Brunello di Montalcino fills the cloisters of Sant'Agostino and the Montalcino fortress with the new five-year-aged Brunello, the Riserva and the freshly released Rosso. A press five-star ceremony assigns a vintage rating before the public days.
seasonal_wine.tip: Book accommodation in Sant'Antimo or Sant'Angelo in Colle to avoid a sold-out Montalcino centre and to be close to the producer cellar visits surrounding the official tasting.

seasonal_wine.name: Bolgheri en primeur, late spring
seasonal_wine.description: The Consorzio Bolgheri pours the new Bolgheri DOC reds and Sassicaia bottlings under barrel-sample conditions for international press. Tenuta San Guido, Ornellaia, Le Macchiole and Grattamacco anchor the show along the Strada del Vino Costa degli Etruschi.
seasonal_wine.tip: Trade-only on most days; consumers can mirror the tasting by booking estate visits via the Strada del Vino Costa degli Etruschi office in Castagneto Carducci.

seasonal_wine.name: Cantine Aperte, last weekend of May
seasonal_wine.description: Movimento Turismo del Vino Toscana opens around 200 member estates across the region on the last weekend of May for cellar visits, paired meals, vineyard hikes and harvest-equipment demos. The 2026 edition runs 30 and 31 May.
seasonal_wine.tip: Many estates open without booking but the top ten Brunello cellars usually require a confirmed slot; check Movimento Turismo del Vino's regional listings two weeks ahead.

seasonal_wine.name: Calici di Stelle, around 10 August
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, late July to mid August
seasonal_wine.description: Movimento Turismo del Vino Toscana hosts vineyard tastings under the Perseid meteor shower on the Night of San Lorenzo (10 August), with estate dinners, telescopes and live music. The 2025 edition spanned 25 July to 24 August across the region's wineries.
what_to_taste: Vernaccia di San Gimignano summer pours
seasonal_wine.tip: Vinci, Montalcino and Castelfalfi run the largest open-air programs; bring a flashlight and a wool wrap, the hills get cool after midnight.

seasonal_wine.name: Vendemmia on the Bolgheri coast, late August to early September
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, late August into early September
seasonal_wine.description: Harvest opens on the Tyrrhenian gravels of Bolgheri with whites first (Vermentino, Sauvignon Blanc), then Merlot from the warmest blocks. Tenuta San Guido, Ornellaia, Masseto and Le Macchiole all begin around the same Bordeaux-grape window.
what_to_taste: Cellar-fresh barrel samples on private visits
seasonal_wine.tip: Late-August Bolgheri visits give the highest chance of seeing pickers in the rows; midweek visits avoid the August Italian holiday crush.

seasonal_wine.name: Bolgheri DiVino, early September
seasonal_wine.description: On the eve of the Bordeaux-grape harvest the Consorzio Bolgheri stages Bolgheri DiVino at the Castello della Gherardesca and Frantoio of Casa Carducci, opening a walk-around tasting of all consortium producers plus a thousand-seat dinner on the cypress-lined Viale dei Cipressi.
seasonal_wine.tip: The Cena dei Mille on Viale dei Cipressi sells out months in advance; tickets release via the Consorzio Bolgheri site each spring.

seasonal_wine.name: Vendemmia in Chianti Classico, mid September to early October
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, mid September to early October
seasonal_wine.description: Sangiovese harvest swings across Chianti Classico from south (Castelnuovo Berardenga) to north (Greve, Rufina, Montefioralle) as altitudes rise. Castello di Ama, Felsina, Fontodi and Isole e Olena typically pick in this window.
what_to_taste: Vintage prelude flights at member estates
seasonal_wine.tip: Most Gallo Nero estates accept harvest visitors by appointment; pair the cellar visit with a vineyard lunch to see crushed must in the open vats.

seasonal_wine.name: Vendemmia in Montalcino, late September to mid October
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, late September to mid October
seasonal_wine.description: Montalcino Sangiovese (locally Brunello) starts later than Chianti Classico thanks to higher altitudes; northern slopes (Montosoli, Torrenieri) finish first, southern Sant'Angelo and Castelnuovo dell'Abate later. The Rosso di Montalcino fruit precedes the Brunello blocks.
what_to_taste: Brunello barrel samples one year prior to release
seasonal_wine.tip: Producers like Il Poggione, Casanova di Neri and Mastrojanni host harvest lunches; ask about the lieu-dit-by-lieu-dit picking calendar to understand the altitude ladder.

seasonal_wine.name: Vino al Vino, Panzano, mid September
seasonal_wine.description: Since 1995 the Unione Viticoltori di Panzano gathers 23 producers in Piazza Bucciarelli, the medieval village square, for a four-day tasting of more than 80 wines. The 2026 edition runs 18 to 20 September.
seasonal_wine.tip: Each producer pours their own bottle range from a stall; buy the tasting glass at the entrance and walk the square clockwise to taste alphabetically.
